Conventional vehicle window mounted antennas and defogger elements typically are connected to a coaxial cable having metal connectors forming male members which are inserted into female connectors coupled to the contact pads on the window. The coaxial cables are typically attached to an electronic module, such as antenna amplifiers, filters, etc., via mechanical pressed together connections formed on loose electrical lead lines. The electrical leads of the cable are first attached to connectors via a crimping/stacking/soldering process. During installation, the male connectors are inserted into the female connectors on the module. This installation process typically requires a degree of skill and strength to matingly seat the connectors. Often the coaxial cable is inserted into the module before installation of the module into the vehicle to facilitate cable/module installation.
